title: Repeat
description:
Given a vector $a\in\mathbb{R}^n$ and a repeat count $d$, return a $[d,n]$ tensor whose every row is a copy of $a$ (tile $a$ along a new leading axis using broadcasting, not an explicit copy loop).

**Shapes**: `a`:[n] -> output:[d,n].
starter_code:
import torch

def repeat(a, d):
    # a: [n], d: scalar repeat count; return [d, n] tiling of a
    # TODO
    pass
hints: Give `a` a leading size-1 axis with `a[None, :]`, then add a `[d, 1]` zero tensor so broadcasting stretches it to `[d, n]`.
1 line, broadcasting only: no `repeat`/`expand`/loop needed, adding zero broadcasts the row across d copies.

solutions.name: Broadcast add of zeros
solutions.explanation: `a[None, :]` has shape `[1, n]`. Adding a `[d, 1]` zero tensor broadcasts both operands to `[d, n]`, replicating the row of `a` down every one of the `d` rows without changing any value.
